purpose: Reference row. Every prompt below already embeds these style rules; this row exists so that a human reviewer or new model has the brand language in one place.
reuse_notes: Used as the baseline for every other row in this CSV.
subject: (n/a)
style_notes: Brand visual language — Unite Medical: editorial documentary photography in the spirit of NYT Magazine, Monocle, Aesop campaigns, and Kinfolk. Shot on 35mm color film (Kodak Portra 400 feel) or medium format. Natural light, slight visible grain, shallow-but-not-bokeh-heavy depth of field. Color palette is warm and restrained: cream paper (#f7f2ea, #ede5d6), ink (#241a28), and small accents of deep plum (#5e2963) only when they appear naturally. Compositions are quiet, slightly off-center, with generous negative space on one side so that headline type can land on top. NO visible logos, NO visible text, NO recognizable celebrities, NO stock-photo gloss, NO fluorescent cyan hospital sheen, NO fake smiles, NO heavy retouching. Faces, when shown, should feel real and slightly weathered — like documentary subjects, not models. Hands and skin tones should be true to a wide range of real American workers. Equipment must look used, not new-in-box. 16:9 aspect ratio unless otherwise specified.
prompt: (see style_notes column)

purpose: Sets the visual thesis of the brand: a real working warehouse, late-afternoon light, calm and confident. Anchors the H1 "The supply chain behind American medicine."
dimensions: 16:9 wide hero, ~1920×1200 source so it crops to 500h on desktop and 280h on mobile. A small white inventory card overlays the bottom-left, so keep the lower-left third of the frame uncluttered.

subject: Wide interior of a medical-supply distribution warehouse at golden hour, end-of-aisle view down a pick path of beige cardboard cases on industrial pallet racks.
style_notes: Editorial documentary, Portra 400 film aesthetic, warm cream-and-amber palette, low contrast. No people. No visible logos.
prompt: Editorial documentary photograph, shot on 35mm color film with Kodak Portra 400, of the interior of a working medical-supply distribution warehouse during late-afternoon golden hour. Camera is at human eye height, looking straight down a single pick path between two industrial pallet-rack runs that recede toward a far wall. The racks are stacked with plain beige and kraft cardboard cases, no visible brand logos, no visible text. Soft warm sunlight rakes in horizontally from a roll-up door at the end of the aisle, casting long warm-amber light on the polished concrete floor and turning the dust in the air visible. Color palette is cream, kraft brown, soft warm amber, with a faint deep-plum shadow under the racks. Composition leaves the lower-left third of the frame relatively quiet so a small UI card can overlay it. No people. Slight 35mm grain. No HDR. No glossy retouching. No fluorescent ceiling glare.

section: Editorial portrait below H1 "Built by a soldier. Run like one."

purpose: Establishes the founder as a real, present operator — not a stock CEO photo. Damon Reed is a 22-year U.S. Army logistics veteran, in his late 40s, founder & CEO of Unite Medical (Lithia Springs, GA). Photo lives directly under the H1 and pairs with a paragraph about his service.

subject: Mid-40s former-Army-logistics man standing on a warehouse floor in Lithia Springs, GA, hands on hips, looking past camera, late-afternoon working light.
style_notes: Editorial documentary portrait, Portra 400 film, warm and quiet, real-feeling. Subject is anonymous (we are casting a body double) — frame so the face is recognizable but does not need celebrity likeness.
prompt: Editorial documentary portrait shot on 35mm Kodak Portra 400 of a man in his mid-to-late 40s, white American, salt-and-pepper hair, weathered hands, wearing a plain dark-navy work polo with no visible logos and well-worn dark jeans. He is standing three-quarter angle to camera in the center aisle of a working medical-supply warehouse, hands relaxed on hips, looking off to camera-left toward a roll-up bay door where late-afternoon golden light rakes in. He is not smiling but is calm and present. Behind him, soft-focus pallet racks of plain kraft cardboard cases. Frame is 16:9 wide, subject sits on the right two-thirds, leaving the left third quiet for headline overlay. Warm cream and amber palette with a small natural plum-purple cast in the deep shadows. Slight film grain. No fluorescent ceiling glare, no HDR, no glossy retouching, no logos, no visible text. Documentary, not corporate.

prompt: Editorial documentary photograph, shot on medium-format film, of a quiet ambulatory-surgery-center operating room mid-turnover. The OR table has been freshly draped with neatly folded light-blue surgical drapes, a stainless instrument tray sits alongside with a clean prep towel, the overhead surgical lights are dimmed and angled away. Late-afternoon golden light filters through a frosted side window at camera-left, washing the polished epoxy floor in a warm amber-and-cream tone. No people. No patients. No blood. No procedure. The room reads as: prepared, calm, ready for the next case. Vertical 3:4 portrait composition with the OR table center-frame. Color palette: pale clinical blue, cream, warm amber light, deep ink shadow. Slight film grain. No HDR, no glossy retouch, no fluorescent cyan glare. No visible logos or text.

prompt: Editorial top-down photograph, medium-format film, of a single sheet of white US-Letter-sized paper laid on a warm-stained walnut desk, photographed straight overhead. The page is laid out like a formal determination letter — generic government-style header block (abstract / illegible), three short paragraphs of body text (abstract, blurred / out-of-focus enough to be unreadable), and a signature block at the bottom. In the right margin, hand-written red-ballpoint notes circle a phrase mid-page and an arrow points to the signature line. Beside the page rests a black resin fountain pen and a small kraft-paper sticky note with a single illegible scribble. Soft warm window light from camera-left. 3:4 portrait composition. Palette: cream paper, warm walnut, red ink, black resin pen. Slight film grain, no HDR, no glossy retouch, no readable text, no real letterhead, no real agency seal.
